title: Global Hydrogen Mobility Alliance: Air Liquide Joins a Strategic Partnership in Europe
content: Air Liquide has joined the newly formed Global Hydrogen Mobility Alliance, a coalition of industry leaders from equipment manufacturing, automotive, technology, energy, and chemical sectors. The Alliance aims to accelerate hydrogen mobility in Europe to strengthen industrial competitiveness and ensure long-term energy sustainability. According to Erwin Penfornis, Vice President of Air Liquideâ€™s Hydrogen Energy business, Europe must diversify its decarbonization approach by promoting hydrogen alongside battery electric vehicles. Hydrogen mobility offers unique advantages such as lower infrastructure demands, better grid resilience, longer range, faster refueling, and suitability for heavy-duty transport. Despite mature and safe technology, adoption is limited due to high costs, underdeveloped refueling networks, and limited vehicle production. Meanwhile, regions like China are rapidly advancing, already deploying tens of thousands of hydrogen vehicles. To catch up, the Alliance proposes a â€œmarket activation phaseâ€ involving coordinated fleet and infrastructure rollouts, policy support like toll exemptions, and a technology-neutral stance. The broader goal is to build a robust, decarbonized hydrogen economy that enhances Europeâ€™s industrial and energy sovereignty. Air Liquide is investing across the value chainâ€”from green hydrogen production to infrastructureâ€”with partners like TotalEnergies to help realize this vision.
author: Vanshika Goyal
